era_context: Early Bronze Age III; Fifth Dynasty Egypt (c. 2494–2345 BCE); Early Dynastic III Mesopotamia; Indus Valley Civilization mature phase (c. 2600–1900 BCE). This era is defined by the consolidation of state-level societies in river valleys, the development of writing systems, long-distance trade, and monumental architecture. Elite power is concentrated in the hands of kings and priests.
disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are attested for 2978 BCE. The year falls within well-documented civilizations, but no sources record particular events for this year.
historiographic_note: The year 2978 BCE has no documented events in the surviving historical record. The sparsity reflects both the limitations of ancient record-keeping and the loss of records over time. Ancient societies did not systematically record events by individual year; our chronological frameworks are modern constructions based on king lists, astronomical calculations, and radiocarbon dating. For the Early Bronze Age, year-specific documentation is essentially non-existent outside of rare astronomical observations or dated administrative records.
